It's not that CPK is a bad professor, but rather Sullivan, who often also teaches another section of the course with her is a really really good professor. (Usually, not this semester). it has gotten better but she used to be terrible I’m currently taking cs112 with CPK and I’m confused on the deeply rooted hatred for her. She seems incredibly genuine, straightforward, and explains topics clearly. She communicates all her expectations and provides more than enough materials to help you succeed. I thought Vahid had left BU? amazing if he hasn't, he truly was amazing, not a CS student, but I took CS111 with him. I heard he was teaching 131 this summer even tho he’s been gone and he's fantastic. BU CS needs more profs like Andrew Wood BU CS overall has a strong focus on theory because it was originally separated from the math department A LONG time ago. This is good if you are looking to get into research, but we do also have many classes that are more application based. These are the cou

t into research, but we do also have many classes that are more application based. ny of the classes, you can use the course search to get the full description/better idea of what you’ll learn: If you don’t know what you want to do with your degree specifically, as you get further into your degree you can take classes from the different groups that may help (Database Vs software engineering vs AI vs data science).
